_comment: Tenant configuration for Ecolaundry / ecolaundry. Single source of truth for behaviour-tuning + business constants. See docs/settings.md for the full field reference.
_section_languages: Active languages for this tenant. defaultLanguage is the fallback when the customer's language can't be detected. enabledLanguages MUST contain defaultLanguage.
_section_branding: Tenant identity strings. Substituted into prompts (welcome, escalation summary, FAQ replies). Both are used by buildSystemPrompt + escalation.ts.
_section_llm: LLM provider tuning. model is the OpenRouter id; agentMaxTokens caps output length per call; maxToolHops caps tool-call iterations per turn. Per-call temperatures are configured in _section_temperatures below.
_section_tone: Free-form tone descriptor injected into agent.txt. Reinforces the prompt's TONE & EMPATHY section. Keep short and concrete.
_section_support_contacts: Email addresses surfaced in FAQ + handover summaries. The 'invoice' inbox handles billing/factura cases (Caso 9); 'support' is the generic operator inbox.
_section_welcome: Per-language welcome line shown only on T1 (rule #G2). Adding a language requires both an entry here AND parity in json/i18n/<lang>.json.

welcomeMessage.es: 👋 Hola, soy el asistente virtual de la lavandería.

_section_session_lifecycle: historyResetTtlMs: when the gap between last assistant message and incoming user message exceeds this, the session is recreated fresh (welcome + re-ask location). Default 3 600 000 (1 hour). sessionIdleTtlMs: in-process session cache eviction (idle > this → evicted to bound memory). Default 1 800 000 (30 minutes). agentSessionCacheMax: hard cap on cached sessions (LRU eviction by lastUsedAt when over cap).
_section_branch_router: When true, T1 messages are classified by utils/router.ts (LLM call) into a branch and dispatched to utils/branches/<branch>/handler.ts. T2+ stay deterministic via state.activeBranch. When false (default), the legacy guard pipeline runs unchanged. See docs/branch-router-architecture.md and TODO.md item #2.
_section_natural_rephrase: When true, every guard outcome that is NOT operator-only structured output is passed through utils/agent-rephrase.ts (LLM polish call) for natural tone-matching, using conversation history as context. Adds ~$0.0005 + ~1s latency per rephrased turn. Content invariants are preserved by the rephrase prompt (sacred rules, keywords, codes). Off by default — opt-in via this flag. See CLAUDE.md Pending refactors D1.
_section_operator_briefing: When true, the operator handover summary (the '**👤 Human Support message**' block) is generated by utils/operator-briefing.ts using the conversation history instead of the deterministic template in utils/escalation.ts:buildEscalationSummary. Produces a more natural narrative for the operator. Adds ~$0.0005 + ~1s latency on the escalation-closure turn only. Strict system prompt forbids invented facts; falls back to the deterministic summary on any LLM error. Off by default — tests run against the deterministic summary so assertions on summary content stay reliable.
_section_temperatures: Per-LLM-call temperatures. Each LLM call site has its own use case: router=classification (low T, anti-hallucination), agent=generation (medium T), rephrase=polish (medium T with strict content constraints), operatorBriefing=summarisation (low T, factual). agentTemperature is the legacy global setting consumed by utils/agent-llm.ts (main turn LLM). routerTemperature drives utils/router.ts (T1 branch classifier — recommended 0-0.2). rephraseTemperature drives utils/agent-rephrase.ts (recommended 0.2-0.5). operatorBriefingTemperature drives utils/operator-briefing.ts (recommended 0-0.3 — keep low to avoid invented facts).
